text.title: market-research-tam-sam-som Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for market-research-tam-sam-som as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Presenting only TAM without a bottom-up SOM calculation
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: TAM is the ceiling; SOM is what determines whether the business is worth building. Investors require both; a TAM-only deck signals the founder has not done the work.

failure-mode.description: Trusting a single industry report source
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Single-source TAMs are the number-one reason investor memos fall apart. Triangulate from at least 3 independent sources and publish the range, not a point estimate.

failure-mode.description: Ignoring competitor share in the SOM calculation
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: SOM without subtracting established competitor share overestimates the realistically capturable market in a competitive category.

failure-mode.description: Static SOM in a fast-moving category
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: SOM must be redrawn every 6 months in high-CAGR categories; a stale curve presented as current breaks credibility in investor Q&A.

failure-mode.description: Agent conflates TAM (gross addressable revenue) with ARR projection (net revenue)
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Both numbers must appear with explicit labels every time; they are not interchangeable and differ by assumptions about market share, pricing, and churn.

failure-mode.description: Tier-mix defaulting to even splits (33/33/33 across Starter/Pro/Enterprise)
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Even splits never match reality; force the agent to read category benchmarks (OpenView, ChartMogul) before assigning tier-mix percentages.

failure-mode.description: Agent puts the same customer account in both PLG and outbound channel buckets
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Each customer appears in exactly one channel bucket; require a uniqueness key per row in the GTM cut model.

failure-mode.description: Agent generates 200 words for the investor slide
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Investor cut is capped at 40 words on the slide; all additional detail goes in the appendix where partners can read it if challenged.

failure-mode.description: Agent cites a hallucinated comparable ("Stripe is at 12% of TAM") without a source URL
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require a URL for every comparable cited on the investor cut; strike any row without one.

failure-mode.description: Agent uses headline list price as tier ARPU rather than effective ARPU
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Effective ARPU = list × (1 - discount) × annual-billing-mix; require an explicit effective-ARPU column, not list price.
